summary: CNN This Morning 报道美国250周年庆典前夕的争议与挑战：特朗普翻新林肯纪念堂倒影池引发批评，前奥运选手因破坏被捕；东北部极端高温威胁独立日庆祝活动，多地调整安排；泰勒·斯威夫特与特拉维斯·凯尔西婚礼周末开始；时代广场海军陆战队表演助兴。

sentences.en: The 24 Marines carried out their trademark rifle routine in perfect sync and without verbal commands, wowing the crowd.
sentences.cn: 24名海军陆战队员以完美的同步和无口令的方式执行了他们标志性的步枪操练，令观众惊叹不已。
sentences.structure: 主句（The 24 Marines carried out...）+ 伴随状语（in perfect sync and without verbal commands）+ 结果状语（wowing the crowd）
sentences.analysis: 现在分词短语'wowing the crowd'作结果状语，表示动作带来的自然结果。'in perfect sync'意为'完美同步'，'without verbal commands'意为'无口令'。

sentences.en: With temperatures expected to top 100 degrees this weekend in the Northeast, it's now -- it's not clear how many people will actually venture out for the big America 250 celebration.
sentences.cn: 由于预计本周末东北部气温将超过100华氏度，目前尚不清楚究竟有多少人会冒险参加盛大的美国250周年庆典。
sentences.structure: With复合结构（With temperatures expected...）+ 主句（it's not clear）+ 主语从句（how many people...）
sentences.analysis: 'With temperatures expected to top...'是独立主格结构，表示原因。'venture out'意为'冒险外出'。破折号表示语气停顿。

sentences.en: In Philly, officials shortened the route of a Thursday morning parade, canceled an afternoon All-American Block Party, and pushed back the start time of an evening picnic and concert at Independence Mall.
sentences.cn: 在费城，官员们缩短了周四上午游行的路线，取消了一场下午的全美街区派对，并推迟了独立购物中心晚间野餐和音乐会的开始时间。
sentences.structure: 地点状语（In Philly）+ 主句（officials shortened..., canceled..., and pushed back...）三个并列谓语
sentences.analysis: 三个并列动词短语（shortened, canceled, pushed back）构成排比，体现一系列调整措施。'pushed back'意为'推迟'。

sentences.en: A former Olympic canoeist has been indicted by a grand jury for allegedly vandalizing it.
sentences.cn: 一名前奥运皮划艇运动员因涉嫌破坏它而被大陪审团起诉。
sentences.structure: 主句（A former Olympic canoeist has been indicted）+ 方式状语（by a grand jury）+ 原因状语（for allegedly vandalizing it）
sentences.analysis: 'has been indicted'是现在完成时的被动语态。'for allegedly vandalizing it'中'allegedly'表示'据称'，体现新闻的客观性。

sentences.en: And we will not allow our sacred monuments to be roped off, defaced or diminished, or in any way impacted by disgruntled individuals who think that they, and not the rest of the nation, have the right to decide what should happen.
sentences.cn: 我们不会允许我们神圣的纪念碑被围起来、污损、贬低，或以任何方式受到心怀不满的个人影响，这些人认为他们——而不是国家其他人民——有权决定应该发生什么。
sentences.structure: 主句（we will not allow...）+ 宾语补足语（to be roped off, defaced or diminished, or impacted）+ 定语从句（who think that...）+ 宾语从句（that they... have the right to decide...）+ 插入语（and not the rest of the nation）
sentences.analysis: 长句包含多个并列的过去分词（roped off, defaced, diminished, impacted）和嵌套从句。'disgruntled'意为'不满的'。插入语'and not the rest of the nation'强调对比。

sentences.en: And I think it probably is now -- you probably have tourists now visiting the Reflecting Pool more than they probably have because of the news coverage from it.
sentences.cn: 我认为现在可能——由于新闻报道，现在参观倒影池的游客可能比以前更多了。
sentences.structure: 主句（I think）+ 宾语从句（it probably is now...）+ 插入语（you probably have tourists...）+ 原因状语（because of the news coverage）
sentences.analysis: 口语化表达，破折号表示思路转折。'more than they probably have'是比较结构，省略了'visited'。

sentences.en: But the issue seems to be that the president is thinking about it and caring about it a lot.
sentences.cn: 但问题似乎在于总统对此想得很多，也很在意。
sentences.structure: 主句（the issue seems to be）+ 表语从句（that the president is thinking... and caring...）
sentences.analysis: 'seems to be that'引导表语从句。'thinking about it and caring about it'两个并列动名词短语，强调总统的关注。

sentences.en: And he spent quite a lot of time on it at a time in this period where we've been at war, where we continue to have whatever is going on in Iran at this point, even with the agreements to supposedly stop fighting.
sentences.cn: 而他在这个时期花了大量时间在这上面，在此期间我们一直处于战争状态，我们在伊朗问题上仍然有各种事态发展，即使有据称能停止战斗的协议。
sentences.structure: 主句（he spent quite a lot of time on it）+ 时间状语（at a time）+ 定语从句（where we've been at war, where we continue to have...）+ 让步状语（even with the agreements...）
sentences.analysis: 两个'where'引导的定语从句修饰'time'，表示多重背景。'whatever is going on'是名词性从句作宾语。'supposedly'意为'据称'。

sentences.en: And that is confusing to a lot of people, I think, when they see that this is -- look, it's a reflection.
sentences.cn: 我认为，当人们看到这——看，它就是一个倒影——时，这会让很多人感到困惑。
sentences.structure: 主句（that is confusing）+ 插入语（I think）+ 时间状语从句（when they see that...）+ 直接引语（look, it's a reflection）
sentences.analysis: 口语化表达，'I think'作插入语。'that this is -- look, it's a reflection'中破折号表示说话者自我纠正或强调。

sentences.en: And what's going to happen on Saturday night on July 4th, is that on the National Mall, the president is going to give a rally speech basically.
sentences.cn: 而7月4日周六晚上将要发生的是，在国家广场，总统基本上要发表一场集会演讲。
sentences.structure: 主语从句（what's going to happen...）+ 系动词（is）+ 表语从句（that on the National Mall, the president is going to give...）
sentences.analysis: 'what's going to happen'是主语从句。'basically'是副词，意为'基本上'，修饰整个表语从句。

sentences.en: And because of that rally speech, not only is it going to be hot, but it's going to push the fireworks to later at night.
sentences.cn: 由于那场集会演讲，不仅天气会很热，而且烟花表演将被推迟到深夜。
sentences.structure: 原因状语（because of that rally speech）+ 并列结构（not only... but also...）
sentences.analysis: 'not only... but...'连接两个并列分句，且'not only'位于句首引起部分倒装（is it going to be hot）。'push... to later'意为'推迟到更晚'。

sentences.en: And instead, families are making the decision, again, because of the heat, but because it doesn't seem like the fireworks will start until after 11 p.m.
sentences.cn: 相反，家庭正在做出决定，再次因为高温，但也因为烟花似乎要到晚上11点之后才开始。
sentences.structure: 主句（families are making the decision）+ 插入语（again）+ 原因状语（because of the heat, but because...）
sentences.analysis: 'again'是插入语，表示重复。'because of... but because...'并列两个原因，第二个是完整的从句。'it doesn't seem like'是口语化表达，相当于'it seems that... not'。

sentences.en: And the Trump administration, when they came in, they said, look, we don't think this group is doing a good job.
sentences.cn: 而特朗普政府在上任时说，看，我们认为这个团体做得不好。
sentences.structure: 主句（the Trump administration said）+ 时间状语从句（when they came in）+ 直接引语（look, we don't think...）
sentences.analysis: 'when they came in'是时间状语从句，'they'指代特朗普政府。'look'是口语中的感叹词，引起注意。

sentences.en: And some of those packages are facetime; like a photo with the president.
sentences.cn: 其中一些套餐包括见面机会；比如与总统合影。
sentences.structure: 主句（some of those packages are facetime）+ 举例（like a photo with the president）
sentences.analysis: 'facetime'此处意为'见面机会'，非视频通话。分号连接两个部分，后一部分举例说明。

topics.title: 美国250周年庆典争议
topics.content: 美国250周年庆典（America 250）因特朗普政府主导的'自由250'（Freedom 250）活动引发政治分歧。批评者认为该活动成为特朗普的'面子工程'（vanity project），而支持者则强调国家团结。庆典包括国家广场的烟花和总统演讲，但因高温和安保问题面临挑战。

topics.title: 林肯纪念堂倒影池翻新风波
topics.content: 特朗普政府翻新林肯纪念堂倒影池（Reflecting Pool）引发争议。翻新使用蓝色密封剂（sealant）导致剥落，且合同被指无竞标（no-bid process）。一名前奥运皮划艇运动员因涉嫌破坏被捕。此前奥巴马时期也曾翻新，但同样问题频出。

topics.title: 极端高温影响独立日庆祝
topics.content: 美国东北部遭遇极端高温（extreme heat），气温预计超过100华氏度（约38摄氏度），迫使多地调整独立日庆祝活动。波士顿将烟花表演提前至下午4点，费城缩短游行路线并取消户外活动。华盛顿国家广场的烟花因总统演讲推迟至晚上11点后。
topics.keywords: extreme heat · Fourth of July · fireworks

topics.title: 泰勒·斯威夫特与特拉维斯·凯尔西婚礼
topics.content: 流行歌手泰勒·斯威夫特（Taylor Swift）与NFL球星特拉维斯·凯尔西（Travis Kelce）的婚礼周末开始，被粉丝称为'美国皇室婚礼'（American royal wedding）。婚礼成为媒体焦点，与国庆庆典形成对比。
topics.keywords: Taylor Swift · Travis Kelce · American royal wedding

quiz.question:
原文中 'vanity project' 最接近的含义是？
（原文：'Freedom 250 is his vanity project.'）
quiz.explanation: vanity project 指为了满足个人虚荣心或提升形象而进行的项目，常含贬义。原文中批评者认为 Freedom 250 是特朗普的 vanity project，即'面子工程'。

quiz.question:
短语 'no-bid process' 在文中的含义是？
（原文：'It didn't go through a no-bid process for contracts.'）
quiz.explanation: no-bid process 指不经竞争性招标直接授予合同，常被批评为缺乏透明度。文中对比奥巴马时期的翻新（有招标）与特朗普时期的做法。

quiz.question:
文中 'flashpoint' 指的是什么？
（原文：'Instead, it has become a flashpoint.'）
quiz.explanation: flashpoint 原指燃点，比喻义为冲突或争议的爆发点。文中倒影池成为政治争议的 flashpoint。

quiz.question: 根据文章，特朗普翻新倒影池与奥巴马时期的主要区别是什么？
options: A. 特朗普使用了更昂贵的材料
B. 特朗普的合同没有经过竞标
C. 奥巴马的项目更成功
D. 特朗普的项目提前完工
quiz.explanation: 文章指出奥巴马时期的翻新通过经济刺激计划拨款，经过招标；而特朗普的翻新使用无竞标合同（no-bid process），且因自设 deadline 导致密封剂剥落。

quiz.question: 极端高温如何影响独立日庆祝活动？
options: B. 波士顿烟花表演改到下午4点
D. 费城增加户外活动
quiz.explanation: 文章提到波士顿的 Pops Fireworks Spectacular 从中午改到下午4点；费城缩短游行路线、取消下午活动并推迟音乐会。华盛顿的烟花因总统演讲推迟到晚上11点后。

quiz.question: 关于泰勒·斯威夫特婚礼，文章表达了什么观点？
options: B. 婚礼被称为美国皇室婚礼
C. 婚礼被批评为浪费
D. 婚礼在华盛顿举行
quiz.explanation: 文章引用粉丝的话称婚礼是 'American royal wedding'，并将其与国庆庆典并列，作为媒体关注的另一焦点。

paragraphs.speaker: Cnn Anchor
paragraphs.text: In New York, crowds in Times Square got an awesome display from the Marines' silent drill platoon. The 24 Marines carried out their trademark rifle routine in perfect sync and without verbal commands, wowing the crowd. This is all part of the celebrations for America's 250th birthday. We have a lot that we'll continue to track over the coming days in that celebration, of course. That does it for CNN HEADLINE EXPRESS. I'm Brad Smith. CNN THIS MORNING with Audie Cornish starts right now.
paragraphs.cn: 在纽约，时代广场的人群欣赏了海军陆战队无声仪仗队的精彩表演。24名海军陆战队员在没有口头指令的情况下，完美同步地执行了他们标志性的步枪操练，令观众惊叹不已。这是美国250岁生日庆祝活动的一部分。当然，在接下来的几天里，我们还会继续追踪这场庆祝活动的许多内容。CNN头条快讯到此结束。我是布拉德·史密斯。奥黛·科尼什主持的《CNN今晨》节目现在开始。

paragraphs.speaker: Audie Cornish
paragraphs.text: Today in the group chat, on the eve of America's 250th birthday, are Trump's celebrations in the capital pushing us further apart?
paragraphs.cn: 今天在群聊中，在美国250岁生日前夕，特朗普在首都的庆祝活动是否让我们更加分裂？

paragraphs.speaker: Unidentified Female
paragraphs.text: Freedom 250 is his vanity project.
paragraphs.cn: 自由250是他的虚荣项目。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Extreme and punishing heat. Will the dangerous weather scorch Fourth of July celebrations? And those Empire State Building climbers now facing federal charges? Why they say they pulled off the death-defying stunt. And it's the big day. Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce reportedly set to get married tonight. The final preparations for the event of the season.
paragraphs.cn: 极端且酷热的高温。危险的天气会破坏七月四日的庆祝活动吗？那些帝国大厦攀爬者现在面临联邦指控？他们为何声称完成了这项玩命的特技？还有，今天是大日子。据报道，泰勒·斯威夫特和特拉维斯·凯尔斯今晚将结婚。本季盛事的最后准备工作。

paragraphs.speaker: Smitty Kovach
paragraphs.text: This is like an all-time wedding. I mean this is like the American royal wedding.
paragraphs.cn: 这简直是世纪婚礼。我的意思是，这就像美国的皇室婚礼。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: On the eve of America's 250th birthday bash, the heat is on. Good morning, everybody. I'm Audie Cornish. We're going to start with a celebration that may be wilting in the blistering summer sun. With temperatures expected to top 100 degrees this weekend in the Northeast, it's now -- it's not clear how many people will actually venture out for the big America 250 celebration. Turnout is already low in D.C. Other cities are making last-minute adjustments.
paragraphs.cn: 在美国250岁生日庆典前夕，热浪来袭。大家早上好。我是奥黛·科尼什。我们从一个可能在烈日下萎靡的庆祝活动开始。由于东北部本周末气温预计将超过100华氏度，目前尚不清楚有多少人会真正冒险参加美国250周年大型庆祝活动。华盛顿特区的出席人数已经很低。其他城市正在做最后一刻的调整。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: In my hometown of Boston, the Pops Fireworks Spectacular will now start at 4 p.m. instead of noon on Saturday. In Philly, officials shortened the route of a Thursday morning parade, canceled an afternoon All-American Block Party, and pushed back the start time of an evening picnic and concert at Independence Mall. Now, of course, it wouldn't be a party here without politics. And we're talking about the Reflecting Pool in front of the Lincoln Memorial, which was supposed to be the centerpiece of America 250.
paragraphs.cn: 在我的家乡波士顿，流行烟花盛会将在周六下午4点开始，而不是中午。在费城，官员缩短了周四上午游行的路线，取消了下午的全美街区派对，并将独立广场的晚间野餐和音乐会的开始时间推迟。当然，没有政治就不成其为派对。我们说的是林肯纪念堂前的倒影池，它本应是美国250周年的核心。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Instead, it has become a flashpoint. A former Olympic canoeist has been indicted by a grand jury for allegedly vandalizing it.
paragraphs.cn: 相反，它成了一个引爆点。一名前奥运会皮划艇选手因涉嫌破坏该池而被大陪审团起诉。

paragraphs.speaker: Jeanine Pirro
paragraphs.text: And we will not allow our sacred monuments to be roped off, defaced or diminished, or in any way impacted by disgruntled individuals who think that they, and not the rest of the nation, have the right to decide what should happen.
paragraphs.cn: 我们不会允许我们神圣的纪念碑被封锁、污损或削弱，或以任何方式受到那些认为他们 --  -- 而非国家其他人民 --  -- 有权决定应该发生什么的不满个人的影响。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: So, today in the group chat, Isaac Dovere, CNN senior reporter; Xochiti Hinojosa, CNN political commentator and former director of public affairs at the DOJ; and Jesse Arm, pollster and vice president of external affairs at the Manhattan Institute.
paragraphs.cn: 所以，今天在群聊中，有CNN高级记者艾萨克·多维尔；CNN政治评论员、前司法部公共事务主任索奇蒂尔·伊诺霍萨；以及曼哈顿研究所民调专家兼对外事务副总裁杰西·阿姆。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: So, I would say it's not like we think people are laying in bed at night being like, "The Reflecting Pool, what went wrong?" It's more like, what is the symbolism of this thing? And Jesse, I want to turn to you, because you were pointing out something that I saw and noticed, as well, which is that back in 2010, using money for the economic stimulus program, the Obama administration did a renovation -- structural renovation of the Reflecting Pool. And people completely flipped out. It didn't go well.
paragraphs.cn: 所以，我认为并不是说人们晚上躺在床上想，'倒影池，哪里出问题了？'更像是，这个东西的象征意义是什么？杰西，我想请你谈谈，因为你指出了我也注意到的一点，那就是早在2010年，奥巴马政府曾用经济刺激计划的资金对倒影池进行了一次结构性翻修。结果人们完全炸了锅。那次翻修并不顺利。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: It took two years. What's different about this one?
paragraphs.cn: 那次翻修花了两年时间。这次有什么不同？

paragraphs.speaker: Jesse Arm
paragraphs.text: Well, yes, two-year renovation, I think $34 million spent. And it didn't go so well. I think you can find a CNN segment from, like, 2012 talking about how there's algae still in the pool.
paragraphs.cn: 嗯，是的，两年翻修，我想花了3400万美元。而且效果并不好。我想你可以找到2012年左右CNN的一段报道，讲的是池子里还有藻类。

paragraphs.speaker: Arm
paragraphs.text: So, this is a difficult problem to solve. I've spent -- you know, we've all spent, I think, a lot of time talking about it. But I do think, to some extent, this is a little bit of, like, maybe cable news directing the conversation toward where we want a lot of the outrage to go and be. I don't see, like, the animating force in even Democratic politics right now being about the Reflecting Pool. My mom watches a lot of cable news.
paragraphs.cn: 所以，这是一个很难解决的问题。我花了 --  -- 你知道，我们都花了很多时间讨论它。但我确实认为，在某种程度上，这有点像有线新闻在引导舆论，把大家的愤怒引向我们希望的方向。我不认为现在连民主党政治的核心动力是关于倒影池的。我妈妈看了很多有线新闻。

paragraphs.speaker: Arm
paragraphs.text: She doesn't like Trump. I'm sure she's very concerned about the algae this weekend.
paragraphs.cn: 她不喜欢特朗普。我敢肯定这个周末她非常担心藻类问题。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Well, then, let me ask this question before you try and make the segment not exist, because we're at the start of the show. One thing that's different is the economic stimulus program was paid for by the taxpayers, right? It didn't go through a no-bid process for contracts. The blue sealant that is peeling off is only put there because Trump thought it would look better ahead of America 250. So again, self- imposed deadline, self-imposed issue. Is it a self-own?
paragraphs.cn: 那么，在你试图让这个片段消失之前，让我问一个问题，因为节目才刚刚开始。一个不同之处在于，经济刺激计划是由纳税人买单的，对吧？合同没有经过不招标程序。现在剥落的蓝色密封胶之所以涂上去，只是因为特朗普认为它会让倒影池在美国250周年纪念前看起来更好看。所以，又是自己设定的截止日期，自己造成的问题。这是自作自受吗？

paragraphs.speaker: Xochiti Hinojosa
paragraphs.text: Well, and I think part of it -- I think you're right that I don't think Americans are sitting around thinking about the Reflecting Pool in Washington,
paragraphs.cn: 嗯，我认为部分原因 --  -- 我认为你说得对，我不认为美国人会闲坐着想华盛顿的倒影池。

paragraphs.speaker: D.C. Cornish
paragraphs.text: Yes. Remember when his motorcade was driving up the center --
paragraphs.cn: 是的。还记得他的车队从中间开过去的时候 --  --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: -- of the Reflecting Pool.
paragraphs.cn: --  -- 倒影池的中间。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: And I was, like, why?
paragraphs.cn: 我当时就想，为什么？

paragraphs.speaker: Hinojosa
paragraphs.text: And I think it probably is now -- you probably have tourists now visiting the Reflecting Pool more than they probably have because of the news coverage from it.
paragraphs.cn: 而且我认为现在可能确实如此 --  -- 由于新闻报道，现在参观倒影池的游客可能比以前更多了。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Yes. Not closely, though, and don't touch it.
paragraphs.cn: 是的。不过别靠太近，也别碰它。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Jeanine Pirro is on it.
paragraphs.cn: 珍妮·皮罗正在报道这件事。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: That's them. Can we show that again? That's them.
paragraphs.cn: 就是他们。能再放一遍吗？就是他们。

paragraphs.speaker: Hinojosa
paragraphs.text: -- may be -- may be prosecuted.
paragraphs.cn: --  -- 可能 --  -- 可能会被起诉。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Getting ready. Raising awareness for an issue, as you said, that nobody asked for or cared about, which is, can the Reflecting Pool look bluer? And the motorcade went on there.
paragraphs.cn: 正在准备。提高人们对一个问题的认识，正如你所说，这个问题没人要求也没人在意，那就是：倒影池能不能变得更蓝？然后车队就开过去了。

paragraphs.speaker: Dovere
paragraphs.text: We keep saying Americans aren't thinking about this, aren't caring about it. But the issue seems to be that the president is thinking about it and caring about it a lot. And he spent quite a lot of time on it at a time in this period where we've been at war, where we continue to have whatever is going on in Iran at this point, even with the agreements to supposedly stop fighting. We've got inflation still high, jobs numbers yesterday that weren't great.
paragraphs.cn: 我们一直说美国人不关心这个，不在乎这个。但问题似乎是总统非常关心和在意这件事。而且他在这个时期花了很多时间在这上面，而这段时间我们一直在打仗，伊朗那边也一直有事，即使有协议说要停火。通货膨胀仍然很高，昨天的就业数据也不理想。

paragraphs.speaker: Dovere
paragraphs.text: And this is becoming not just an ongoing problem for the president, but something that he clearly is going to continue spending time on and also is going to continue spending taxpayer money on.
paragraphs.cn: 这不仅是总统的一个持续问题，而且显然他会继续在这上面花时间，也会继续花纳税人的钱。

paragraphs.speaker: Dovere
paragraphs.text: And that is confusing to a lot of people, I think, when they see that this is -- look, it's a reflection. I'm not a pool specialist, but --
paragraphs.cn: 我想这让很多人感到困惑，当他们看到这 --  -- 看，这只是个倒影。我不是水池专家，但 --  --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Yes. No, that's OK. But the only reason I'm asking, once it gets into the legal system, we're now turning something --
paragraphs.cn: 是的。不，没关系。但我问的唯一原因是，一旦进入法律体系，我们就把事情变成了 --  --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: -- that didn't need to be here where it is.
paragraphs.cn: --  -- 这本来不必出现在这里。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: I want to ask about another thing, which is the divisions over America 250 during Freedom 250, I think are kind of telling. This could have been a bipartisan project. And the Trump administration, when they came in, they said, look, we don't think this group is doing a good job. We think they're wasting money. We don't think they're doing things on time. We're going to be taking some of that money.
paragraphs.cn: 我想问另一件事，那就是在"自由250"期间围绕"美国250"的分歧，我认为这很能说明问题。这本可以是一个两党合作的项目。特朗普政府上台后，他们说，看，我们认为这个组织做得不好。我们认为他们在浪费钱。我们认为他们没有按时完成任务。我们将拿走部分资金。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: But in the process, they've also been selling these, like, sponsorship packages, right? And some of those packages are facetime; like a photo with the president.
paragraphs.cn: 但在这个过程中，他们也在出售这些赞助套餐，对吧？其中一些套餐包括与总统合影之类的会面机会。

paragraphs.speaker: Dovere
paragraphs.text: And what's going to happen on Saturday night on July 4th, is that on the National Mall, the president is going to give a rally speech basically. Right? And because of that rally speech, not only is it going to be hot, but it's going to push the fireworks to later at night. So, this thing that -- you know, fireworks are the -- what Americans unite around on July 4th.
paragraphs.cn: 7月4日周六晚上，总统将在国家广场发表一场集会演讲，对吧？因为这场集会演讲，不仅天气会很热，而且烟花表演将被推迟到深夜。所以，这件事 --  -- 你知道，烟花是7月4日美国人团结的象征。

paragraphs.speaker: Dovere
paragraphs.text: And instead, families are making the decision, again, because of the heat, but because it doesn't seem like the fireworks will start until after 11 p.m.
paragraphs.cn: 相反，家庭正在做出决定，同样因为天气炎热，而且烟花似乎要到晚上11点后才开始。

paragraphs.speaker: Dovere
paragraphs.text: To not go to the fireworks --
paragraphs.cn: 不去看烟花 --  --

paragraphs.speaker: Dovere
paragraphs.text: -- and then dealing with the fact that the kids are going to get woken up by the fireworks.
paragraphs.cn: --  -- 然后还要处理孩子被烟花吵醒的问题。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Here's what Trump has to say. Him and also D.C. Mayor Muriel Bowser. They're talking about the heat here, and there are concerns for the celebrations.
paragraphs.cn: 以下是特朗普的说法。他和华盛顿特区市长穆里尔·鲍泽都在谈论这里的高温，以及对庆祝活动的担忧。

paragraphs.speaker: Donald Trump
paragraphs.text: On July 4th, it's going to be approximately 107 degrees out. And I'm going to go, and I'm going to make a really long speech just to show that I can do anything. MAYOR MURIEL BOWSER (D),
paragraphs.cn: 7月4日，气温将高达约107华氏度。我会去，我会做一个很长的演讲，只是为了证明我什么都能做。华盛顿特区市长穆里尔·鲍泽（民主党）：

paragraphs.speaker: Washington
paragraphs.text: The thing to remember, especially with this heat, is that's a long, long day. So, people have to really watch the heat, watch what they're drinking and, and get home as soon as the fireworks are over.
paragraphs.cn: 要记住的是，尤其是在这种高温下，这一天会非常漫长。所以，人们必须注意防暑，注意饮水，烟花一结束就尽快回家。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: In the end, maybe this is moot, because it's just too hot out. No, honestly, like, it's like dangerous. You're not --
paragraphs.cn: 最后，也许这都无关紧要，因为外面实在太热了。不，说真的，简直危险。你不会 --  --

paragraphs.speaker: Dovere
paragraphs.text: Even coming in this morning, I was struck by hot it was already.
paragraphs.cn: 就连今天早上来的时候，我都惊讶于天气已经这么热了。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: No backpacks. Only a certain kind of water bottle.
paragraphs.cn: 不能带背包。只能带特定种类的水瓶。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: There's going to be no folding chairs. No this, no that. I understand the security concerns. But I think the reality is, if you're a person with any frailties whatsoever or, you know, lungs, you look at these triple digits; and you don't think, Let me -- let me go sit out there for what he himself has described, a very long speech.
paragraphs.cn: 不能带折叠椅。这个不行，那个也不行。我理解安全方面的考虑。但我认为现实是，如果你有任何身体不适，或者，你知道，有肺，看到这三四十度的高温，你不会想：让我 --  -- 让我去外面坐那么久，听他本人所说的，一个非常长的演讲。

paragraphs.speaker: Arm
paragraphs.text: Yes. And it's obviously not Trump's fault it's going to be hot outside.
paragraphs.cn: 是的。而且外面天气热显然不是特朗普的错。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: No. That's why I'm showing the map, my man.
paragraphs.cn: 不。所以我给你看地图，老兄。

paragraphs.speaker: Arm
paragraphs.text: Well, no, this is the thing. I think, you know, on the one hand it's a little bit of a challenge, because it's like the president shouldn't spend public tax dollars doing things to, like, make the Reflecting Pool blue or get rid of the algae, or improving parks, or redeveloping fountains.
paragraphs.cn: 嗯，不，问题就在这里。我觉得，你知道，一方面这有点挑战性，因为总统不应该花公共税款去做诸如让倒影池变蓝、清除藻类、改善公园或重建喷泉之类的事情。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: No, we said aesthetic versus structural.
paragraphs.cn: 不，我们说的是美观与结构的问题。

paragraphs.speaker: Arm
paragraphs.text: But on the other hand, it's also like Trump shouldn't seek out private sponsorships for some of these events in America 250 [SIC]. So, I do feel like there's a little bit of a push and pull there. But --
paragraphs.cn: 但另一方面，特朗普也不应该为美国250周年的一些活动寻求私人赞助。所以我确实觉得这里有点矛盾。但是 --  --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: But you mean -- you're saying critics are saying those things.
paragraphs.cn: 但你的意思是 --  -- 你是说批评者在说那些话。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: I can't tell if you're saying those things or critics.
paragraphs.cn: 我分不清是你在说这些话还是批评者在说。

paragraphs.speaker: Arm
paragraphs.text: I'm not saying those things. Critics are pointing those things out. I don't really take issue with either using some amount of public funds to do things like improve fountains in D.C., get them working again that haven't been after a long time, or to seek out private sponsorships for some of these American 250 [SIC] events. The downside, though -- and Republicans should understand this -- is that -- and this leads into the Freedom 250 versus America 250 tension.
paragraphs.cn: 我没说那些话。是批评者指出的。我并不反对用一些公共资金来改善华盛顿特区的喷泉，让它们重新运转起来，或者为一些美国250周年活动寻找私人赞助。但不利的一面 --  -- 共和党人应该明白 --  -- 是 --  -- 这导致了"自由250"和"美国250"之间的紧张关系。

paragraphs.speaker: Arm
paragraphs.text: It's hard to get Hollywood celebrities and stars to come do performances when a Republican is leading the show in Washington, D.C. I would have probably just said from the get-go, it's going to be really difficult to do that. Let's bring in the military bands. Let's bring in the flyovers and let's do this.
paragraphs.cn: 当共和党人在华盛顿主导活动时，很难请到好莱坞明星来表演。我可能从一开始就会说，这很难做到。让我们请军乐队，安排飞越表演，就这样做。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Yes, yes. And we should say we're reporting, actually, from a Democrat claim in the House, that the organizers may have redirected funds or duped donors -- that's the accusation from Democrats right now -- into putting money into the Freedom 250 coffers. You're going to say?
paragraphs.cn: 是的，是的。我们应该说明，我们实际上是在报道众议院民主党人的说法，即组织者可能挪用了资金或欺骗了捐赠者 --  -- 这是民主党人目前的指控 --  -- 将资金投入"自由250"的金库。你想说什么？

paragraphs.speaker: Hinojosa
paragraphs.text: Well, I mean, it actually wouldn't be hard to have celebrities or -- I don't know -- Democratic governors or senators and have it be a bipartisan event if the president, from the beginning, tried to make it a bipartisan event. You saw what he said. He's making this an endurance speech. It's, like, to show that he is some strong man.
paragraphs.cn: 嗯，我的意思是，如果总统从一开始就试图让活动成为两党合作的活动，那么请来名人或 --  -- 我不知道 --  -- 民主党州长或参议员并不难。你看到了他说的话。他正在把这次演讲变成一场耐力演讲。就像是为了显示他是个强人。

paragraphs.speaker: Hinojosa
paragraphs.text: And that's how he is. Right?
paragraphs.cn: 他就是那样，对吧？

paragraphs.speaker: Hinojosa
paragraphs.text: And it started with the UFC fight. It was his 80th birthday. It wasn't a celebration at 250. It was the president's 80th birthday party. This is what it seems like now. There is a speech that the president will, as Isaac said, will -- it will become a MAGA rally campaign speech versus a unifying speech bringing everybody together.
paragraphs.cn: 而且从UFC比赛就开始了。那是他的80岁生日。这不是250周年的庆祝活动，而是总统的80岁生日派对。现在看起来就是这样。总统将发表演讲，正如艾萨克所说，这将变成一场MAGA集会式的竞选演讲，而不是团结所有人的演讲。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: We don't know what it will be yet. What we do know, what it will be --
paragraphs.cn: 我们还不知道会是什么样。但我们知道的是 --  --

paragraphs.speaker: Hinojosa
paragraphs.text: I have yet to see the president make one unifying speech.
paragraphs.cn: 我还没见过总统发表过任何团结的演讲。

paragraphs.speaker: Arm
paragraphs.text: We should point out that the Democratic governors were invited. Even the Hollywood superstars were invited.
paragraphs.cn: 我们应该指出，民主党州长被邀请了，甚至好莱坞巨星也被邀请了。

paragraphs.speaker: Hinojosa
paragraphs.text: But they pulled out, because a lot of -- this is - (CROSSTALK)
paragraphs.cn: 但他们退出了，因为很多 --  -- 这是 --  -- （交叉对话）

paragraphs.speaker: Arm
paragraphs.text: Well, they pulled -- they pulled out.
paragraphs.cn: 嗯，他们退出了 --  -- 他们退出了。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Can I -- can I interrupt? There's something to what she's saying about the fact that Trump stays on message, and his message is rarely a unifying one.
paragraphs.cn: 我能 --  -- 我能打断一下吗？她说的有一点是对的，特朗普坚持自己的信息，而他的信息很少是团结的。

paragraphs.speaker: Arm
paragraphs.text: But the president wasn't going to speak.
paragraphs.cn: 但总统原本不打算发言。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: And now -- I know. But now he's decided to speak extra long. I didn't need that.
paragraphs.cn: 现在 --  -- 我知道。但现在他决定讲得特别长。我不需要那样。

paragraphs.speaker: Arm
paragraphs.text: Because the star -- people pulled out.
paragraphs.cn: 因为明星 --  -- 人们退出了。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: No, send Lee Greenwood out there. That's fine. OK, listen, stay with me. We're going to talk about a couple of things. One quite serious, which is that immigration enforcement continues. We're talking about thousands of arrests in just a handful of days. Why ICE is keeping it on the D.L. Plus, Palm Beach Airport about to get the Trump rebrand. How the president could profit from that. And in the meantime, that skyscraper-scaling daredevil couple. Well, they've been charged with a couple of felonies.
paragraphs.cn: 不，让李·格林伍德上台。那没问题。好的，听着，别挂断。我们要谈几件事。一件相当严肃，即移民执法仍在继续。我们说的是短短几天内数千次逮捕。为什么移民和海关执法局对此保密。另外，棕榈滩机场即将进行特朗普品牌重塑。总统如何从中获利。与此同时，那对攀爬摩天大楼的冒险情侣。嗯，他们被指控犯有几项重罪。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Do prosecutors have a case?
paragraphs.cn: 检察官有证据吗？

paragraphs.speaker: Unidentified Male
paragraphs.text: Based off my reading of this complaint, the D.A.'s office overcharged here. It's nice to have somebody wearing black bats doing a message of peace instead of a -- you know, a message of -- of -- of destruction and war. And, you know, no one was hurt here.
paragraphs.cn: 根据我对这份诉状的解读，地方检察官办公室这里指控过度了。有人穿着黑色蝙蝠装传递和平信息，而不是 --  -- 你知道 --  -- 破坏和战争的信息。而且，你知道，没有人受伤。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: So, it is now 15 minutes past the hour. This is your morning roundup. In Iran, they are paying tribute to former supreme leader Ali Khamenei with a weeklong funeral across five cities. He was killed in February, on the first day of U.S.-Israeli strikes on Iran. The big question this morning: will his son, Mojtaba Khamenei, the new supreme leader, make an appearance? He was injured in the same attack that killed his father; other members of his family, as well. He has not been seen in public since.
paragraphs.cn: 所以，现在是整点过15分。这是你的早间新闻汇总。在伊朗，他们正在为前最高领袖阿里·哈梅内伊举行为期一周、跨越五个城市的葬礼。他于二月在美国和以色列对伊朗发动袭击的第一天被杀。今早的大问题是：他的儿子、新任最高领袖穆杰塔巴·哈梅内伊会露面吗？他在导致父亲死亡的同一袭击中受伤；他的其他家庭成员也受了伤。自那以后，他再也没有公开露面。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: And travelers heading to Palm Beach International Airport getting an early look at a major rebranding. Overnight, new I-95 signs went up, renaming the hub "President Donald J. Trump International Airport." The signage went up ahead of the official name switch, which is set for July 9th. And that influencer couple who scaled the top of the Empire State Building to get engaged, are speaking out about why they did it and what that banner meant.
paragraphs.cn: 前往棕榈滩国际机场的旅客提前看到了重大品牌重塑。一夜之间，新的I-95标志牌竖起，将该枢纽更名为"唐纳德·J·特朗普总统国际机场"。这些标志牌在正式更名之前就已安装，正式更名定于7月9日。还有那对爬上帝国大厦顶端求婚的网红情侣，正在公开谈论他们为何这样做以及那条横幅的含义。

paragraphs.speaker: Unidentified Male
paragraphs.text: Why did you choose that message?
paragraphs.cn: 你们为什么选择那个信息？

paragraphs.speaker: Ivan Beerkus
paragraphs.text: Because we believe in love.
paragraphs.cn: 因为我们相信爱情。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: We also have new details this morning about how they pulled it off. Authorities say video showing the pair hid in the observatory before it closed. Then, in the early dawn hours, they popped through a hatch, cut through the locks to ascend to the top of the spire. They're now facing felony charges of burglary, reckless endangerment, and criminal mischief. The mischief. The group chat is back. Do we believe in love?
paragraphs.cn: 今天早上我们还有关于他们如何成功的新细节。当局称，视频显示这对情侣在观景台关闭前藏身其中。然后，在黎明时分，他们从舱门钻出，剪断锁具，登上了尖塔顶端。他们现在面临盗窃、鲁莽危害和刑事恶作剧的重罪指控。恶作剧。群聊又回来了。我们相信爱情吗？

paragraphs.speaker: Hinojosa
paragraphs.text: We believe in love. I'm not sure I would scale the Empire State Building in order to -- I don't think that is a proposal.
paragraphs.cn: 我们相信爱情。但我不确定我会为了求婚而爬帝国大厦 --  -- 我不认为那是求婚。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: You don't believe in using city resources to believe in love?
paragraphs.cn: 你不相信利用城市资源来相信爱情？

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: This is actually -- I learned this is the first time they've actually been criminally charged. They've done this in other countries. They almost always get let go.
paragraphs.cn: 实际上，我了解到这是他们第一次被刑事指控。他们在其他国家也做过这种事，但几乎总是被释放。

paragraphs.speaker: Dovere
paragraphs.text: Doesn't this smell like a little bit, like, preplanned, like we're all supposed to be talking about this? They all made their way up to the top.
paragraphs.cn: 这难道不有点像是预谋好的，好像我们都应该谈论这件事？他们都爬到了顶端。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Yes. It's a stunt. That's what they do.
paragraphs.cn: 是的，这是一个噱头。他们就是干这个的。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: I just don't know if I should have to pay for -- like, should cities have to expend resources on the stunt?
paragraphs.cn: 我只是不知道我是否应该为此买单 --  -- 比如，城市是否应该为这种噱头耗费资源？

paragraphs.speaker: Arm
paragraphs.text: Oh, no. I'm -- I'm for bringing down the hammer on -- on the Empire State scalers.
paragraphs.cn: 哦，不。我 --  -- 我支持严厉打击那些 --  -- 那些攀爬帝国大厦的人。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Start to the day. We don't believe in love. Godspeed to that company -- that couple. And enjoy your engagement. After the break on CNN THIS MORNING, in the words of "Hamilton," who lives, who dies, who tells your story? How will Americans choose to tell our story on the nation's 250th birthday? Plus, we're going to talk about the speculation, the anticipation. Are Taylor and Travis getting ready to walk down the aisle? Good morning to you all. It is extremely hot out.
paragraphs.cn: 新的一天开始了。我们不相信爱情。祝那家公司 --  -- 那对情侣好运。享受你们的订婚吧。在CNN《今晨》节目广告之后，用《汉密尔顿》的话来说，谁活着，谁死去，谁来讲述你的故事？在美国250岁生日之际，美国人将如何选择讲述我们的故事？另外，我们还要谈谈那些猜测和期待。泰勒和特拉维斯是不是准备步入婚姻殿堂了？大家早上好。外面非常热。

paragraphs.speaker: Unidentified Female
paragraphs.text: Everything with MSG is, like, a whole decoy. And really, they're going to have everyone on jets going elsewhere. Where, I don't know, but I think it's almost -- it's a little too obvious at this point.
paragraphs.cn: 麦迪逊广场花园的一切都像是个幌子。实际上，他们会让大家坐飞机去别的地方。去哪里我不知道，但我觉得这几乎 --  -- 现在有点太明显了。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: OK, conspiracy I can get down with: where are Taylor and Travis going to get married? They might actually be getting married at Madison Square Garden. The wedding weekend seems to be in full swing. Last night there were tents set up outside the Garden, a steady stream of large SUVs seen arriving, reportedly for the rehearsal dinner. For Swifties, the anticipation has reached new heights.
paragraphs.cn: 好吧，我能接受的阴谋论是：泰勒和特拉维斯会在哪里结婚？他们可能真的会在麦迪逊广场花园结婚。婚礼周末似乎正在如火如荼地进行。昨晚花园外面搭起了帐篷，可以看到大量大型SUV陆续抵达，据说是为了彩排晚宴。对于霉粉来说，期待值已经达到了新的高度。

paragraphs.speaker: Unidentified Female
paragraphs.text: We are just team Taylor and Travis for life. Love Travis, so it's really exciting. I'm really happy for them.
paragraphs.cn: 我们永远支持泰勒和特拉维斯。爱特拉维斯，所以真的很兴奋。我为他们感到高兴。

paragraphs.speaker: Unidentified Female
paragraphs.text: Oh my gosh, all the Taylor Swift, all the Swifties out here rooting her on. Come on. She's going to be a bride. It's the best.
paragraphs.cn: 天哪，所有的泰勒·斯威夫特，所有的霉粉都在这里为她加油。加油。她就要当新娘了。这太棒了。

paragraphs.speaker: Kovach
paragraphs.text: This is like the American royal wedding. We've all seen her love stories over the years and really rooted for some over others. And we're really happy that that she found her forever.
paragraphs.cn: 这就像是美国的皇室婚礼。这些年来我们都见证了她的爱情故事，并且真心支持其中一些。我们很高兴她找到了她的永恒。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Can't say it better than that, but we found someone who might be able to. Ralphie Aversa, "USA Today" senior entertainment correspondent. Good morning. How are you doing? I know it's been a long couple of days. RALPHIE AVERSA, SENIOR ENTERTAINMENT CORRESPONDENT, "USA TODAY": Oh, my. Oh, please. I saw you talking about the scalers. I was actually on my way to Madison Square Garden earlier this week to kind of check out the perimeter of the world's most famous arena when that happened.
paragraphs.cn: 说得再好不过了，但我们找到了一个可能说得更好的人。《今日美国》资深娱乐记者拉尔菲·阿维萨。早上好。你好吗？我知道这几天很漫长。拉尔菲·阿维萨，《今日美国》资深娱乐记者：哦，天哪。哦，拜托。我看到你们在谈论那些攀爬者。实际上，本周早些时候我正要去麦迪逊广场花园看看这个世界最著名场馆的周边情况，结果就发生了那件事。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: And I ended up running down 33rd Street to see what was going on. So, Audie, it's been a week, but it's always great to join you.
paragraphs.cn: 我最后跑到了33街去看发生了什么。所以，奥迪，这一周很漫长，但能和你一起总是很棒。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Did you see anyone? We were hearing reports. Well, Selena Gomez actually posted on Instagram -- on Instagram, I think, from the back of an SUV. But we mentioned some names: Jack Antonoff, Lena Dunham, other people who we're actually now seeing; some besties we're seeing outside the building.
paragraphs.cn: 你看到什么人了吗？我们听到了报道。嗯，赛琳娜·戈麦斯实际上在Instagram上发了帖子 --  -- 在Instagram上，我想是从一辆SUV的后座发的。但我们提到了一些名字：杰克·安东诺夫、莉娜·邓纳姆，还有其他我们现在确实看到的人；我们看到一些闺蜜在大楼外面。

paragraphs.speaker: Aversa
paragraphs.text: "USA Today" can confirm that Patrick Mahomes is in the New York City area. Of course, quarterback for the Kansas City Chiefs, who's thrown Travis Kelce quite a few touchdowns. "USA Today" can also confirm that Steve Spagnuolo -- here we are talking about Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce. And we're talking about the coaching staff, as well. The defensive coordinator for the Chiefs is also in the New York City area. He was spotted in midtown Manhattan with his wife.
paragraphs.cn: "今日美国"可以证实帕特里克·马霍姆斯正在纽约市地区。当然，他是堪萨斯城酋长队的四分卫，曾多次给特拉维斯·凯尔斯传出达阵球。"今日美国"也可以证实史蒂夫·斯帕努奥洛 --  -- 我们正在谈论泰勒·斯威夫特和特拉维斯·凯尔斯。我们也在谈论教练组。酋长队的防守协调员也在纽约市地区。有人看到他和妻子在曼哈顿中城。

paragraphs.speaker: Aversa
paragraphs.text: Of course, he had two stints coaching with the New York football Giants, as well. So, yes, we are seeing a number of their celebrity friends, a number of their sport friends, and of course, some family members, as well, all being spotted throughout the tri-state area as we gear up for what we are thinking is -- all signs point to -- a wedding celebration tonight at Madison Square Garden.
paragraphs.cn: 当然，他也曾两次执教纽约巨人队。所以，是的，我们看到他们的许多名人朋友、体育界朋友，当然还有一些家人，都在三州地区被目击，因为我们正在为今晚在麦迪逊广场花园举行的婚礼庆典做准备 --  -- 所有迹象都指向这一点。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: I was hearing that they've actually done a bunch of donations ahead of this wedding to -- in a bunch of different cities. I'm going to throw some images up so people can see where this money was going to, upwards of $26 million. This is actually something I feel like I've seen with some other celebrities, as well. Can you talk about this trend?
paragraphs.cn: 我听说他们在婚礼前向多个城市进行了大量捐赠。我将展示一些图片，让大家看到这些钱的去向，总额高达2600万美元。这实际上是我在其他一些名人身上也看到过的。你能谈谈这个趋势吗？

paragraphs.speaker: Aversa
paragraphs.text: Yes. Well, we can talk about the $26 million in the numerology there: 26, 13 plus 13. Two 13 joining, Taylor's lucky number. So, of course, we have to break that down, as well. I did think the timing of this, though, Audie, was also interesting. You know, this news broke around the time that Staten Island Congresswoman Nicole Malliotakis came out and criticized Taylor and Travis for having this wedding on a holiday weekend.
paragraphs.cn: 是的。我们可以谈谈数字命理学中的2600万：26，13加13。两个13结合，泰勒的幸运数字。所以，当然，我们也得分析一下。不过，奥黛，我认为这个时机也很有趣。你知道，这个消息大约在斯塔滕岛众议员妮可·马利奥塔基斯批评泰勒和特拉维斯在假日周末举行婚礼的时候传出的。

paragraphs.speaker: Aversa
paragraphs.text: And thus, a number of New York police officers will now be working overtime and through the holiday weekend. Obviously, there's a lot already happening in this area with the World Cup and with all of the America 250 celebrations. So, yes, we have been seeing this with other celebrities. And we saw it with Taylor and Travis here. A number of different charities, national and local, to some of the different areas that they have worked and lived in.
paragraphs.cn: 因此，许多纽约警察现在将加班工作，度过这个假日周末。显然，这个地区已经有很多事情发生，比如世界杯和所有美国250周年庆典。所以，是的，我们在其他名人身上也看到了这一点。在泰勒和特拉维斯这里也看到了。他们向全国和地方的一些慈善机构捐款，这些机构与他们工作和生活过的不同地区有关。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: One other thing I want to play for you: Swifties who think they've seen the first images of the decor. Take a listen. We'll talk on the other side.
paragraphs.cn: 还有一件事我想给你播放：斯威夫特粉丝们认为他们看到了装饰的第一批图像。听一下。我们稍后再谈。

paragraphs.speaker: Unidentified Male
paragraphs.text: First images of Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce's wedding decorations. And look at these roses. Look at the color. Madison Square Garden is all decked out in rose blush. That's right, best friend. The color of the season is now rose blush.
paragraphs.cn: 泰勒·斯威夫特和特拉维斯·凯尔斯婚礼装饰的第一批图像。看看这些玫瑰。看看颜色。麦迪逊广场花园完全装饰成了玫瑰红。没错，最好的朋友。这个季节的颜色现在是玫瑰红。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: OK, so is any of this true? I don't know what to do with that. I think I've seen drone images that are a little more impactful, but people are looking for any clue. And you know, it's interesting. I don't ascribe to the royal wedding aspect. America does not have royals. I'm OK saying that around Independence Day. But because it's football and music, like there's something about that, right? In our culture where this is somehow the kind of pinnacle of a kind of success.
paragraphs.cn: 好吧，这些是真的吗？我不知道该怎么处理。我想我看过一些更有冲击力的无人机图像，但人们在寻找任何线索。你知道，这很有趣。我不认同皇室婚礼的说法。美国没有皇室。在独立日前后这么说我觉得没问题。但因为这是足球和音乐，所以有点特别，对吧？在我们的文化中，这似乎是某种成功的巅峰。

paragraphs.speaker: Aversa
paragraphs.text: And the two reported marrying at a venue that marries sports and entertainment quite a bit, with the Knicks, and the Rangers, and college sports, and all of the different concerts and other events that have been held at this venue in midtown Manhattan. So, absolutely, I agree 100 percent. It's funny you talk about drones.
paragraphs.cn: 据报道，这对新人在一个经常结合体育和娱乐的场馆结婚，尼克斯队、游骑兵队、大学体育以及各种音乐会和活动都在曼哈顿中城的这个场馆举行。所以，当然，我百分之百同意。你说到无人机很有趣。

paragraphs.speaker: Aversa
paragraphs.text: You know, again, when these rumors started bubbling up, and we started hearing about them probably about a month and a half ago at this point, I was not shocked, in part because Taylor and Travis and their wedding party, you know, in part won't have to worry about drones snooping in on their wedding.
paragraphs.cn: 你知道，再说一次，当这些谣言开始流传，我们大约在一个半月前开始听说时，我并不震惊，部分原因是泰勒和特拉维斯以及他们的婚礼团队，你知道，部分原因是不用担心无人机偷拍他们的婚礼。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: OK. Ralphie, always a joy to talk to you. I hope you're not just stuck out there in the heat waiting to see it all, but thank you for being with us.
paragraphs.cn: 好的，拉尔菲，和你聊天总是很愉快。希望你不是被困在酷热中等着看这一切，但感谢你与我们同在。

paragraphs.speaker: Aversa
paragraphs.text: I'll stay hydrated. Thanks, Audie.
paragraphs.cn: 我会保持水分。谢谢，奥蒂。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: The other person who will stay hydrated: Elizabeth Wagmeister, who will be outside Madison Square Garden for CNN. She's going to break down all the details of the expected wedding. We're talking venues, ceremony, dress. Guest performances. And there's going to be a special CNN All Access event. It's called "Love Story: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce." That's going to air at 1 p.m. Eastern at CNN.com/Watch. Or you can hit us up on the CNN app.
paragraphs.cn: 另一位会保持水分的是伊丽莎白·瓦格迈斯特，她将在麦迪逊广场花园外为CNN报道。她将解析这场备受期待的婚礼的所有细节。我们将讨论场地、仪式、礼服、嘉宾表演。还将有一场特别的CNN全通道活动，名为"爱情故事：泰勒·斯威夫特和特拉维斯·凯尔西"。该节目将于东部时间下午1点在CNN.com/Watch播出，或者你也可以在CNN应用上观看。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: OK, straight ahead on the show this morning, underdog or frontrunner? Is Jon Ossoff becoming the Democrat to watch in his midterm race? Plus, brutal temperatures setting up for a sweltering Fourth. How it is impacting the celebrations where you are. var CNN = CNN || {};
paragraphs.cn: 好的，今天早上的节目即将开始，是黑马还是领跑者？乔恩·奥索夫会成为中期选举中值得关注的民主党人吗？此外，酷热的气温为闷热的七月四日奠定了基础。这如何影响你所在地区的庆祝活动。

paragraphs.speaker: Audie Cornish
paragraphs.text: Good morning. I'm Audie Cornish. Thank you for joining me on CNN THIS MORNING. It's now half past the hour. And here's what's happening right now. That dangerous heat wave tightening its grip on the eastern U.S., leaving more than 160 million Americans under extreme heat risk today. Temperatures will soar near 100 degrees in major cities, including New York, Philadelphia, Boston, and here in D.C. with a feels like temperature of a brutal 110.
paragraphs.cn: 早上好。我是奥蒂·科尼什。感谢您收看《CNN今晨》。现在是半点钟。以下是正在发生的事情。危险的热浪正在收紧对美国东部的控制，今天有超过1.6亿美国人面临极端高温风险。主要城市的气温将飙升至接近100华氏度，包括纽约、费城、波士顿以及华盛顿特区，体感温度高达残酷的110华氏度。

paragraphs.speaker: Audie Cornish
paragraphs.text: That means parades and fireworks have been canceled or postponed from Delaware to New Jersey, New York and Pennsylvania. And Energy Secretary Chris Wright directed all data centers in the mid-Atlantic to use their backup power supplies instead of drawing electricity from the public grid that way there's enough power for residential air conditioning. And in Venezuela they're celebrating a miraculous rescue more than a week after those devastating earthquakes.
paragraphs.cn: 这意味着从特拉华州到新泽西州、纽约州和宾夕法尼亚州的游行和烟花表演已被取消或推迟。能源部长克里斯·赖特指示中大西洋地区的所有数据中心使用备用电源，而不是从公共电网取电，以确保有足够的电力供住宅空调使用。在委内瑞拉，人们正在庆祝一场奇迹般的救援，距离那些毁灭性的地震已过去一周多。

paragraphs.speaker: Audie Cornish
paragraphs.text: A mall security guard was pulled from the rubble Thursday. Officials say he survived in an air pocket. His rescue comes as the death toll continues to rise. More than 2,200 victims have been found so far. And today, Pope Leo XIV will accept the Philadelphia Liberty Medal. The pope, who's been honored for promoting liberty around the world, will attend the ceremony virtually. Leo is the first American pope. He will not visit the U.S. at all during its 250th year, despite the president's invitation.
paragraphs.cn: 周四，一名商场保安被从废墟中救出。官员称他在一个气穴中幸存。他的获救之际，死亡人数持续上升。目前已发现超过2200名遇难者。今天，教皇利奥十四世将接受费城自由勋章。这位因在全球促进自由而受到表彰的教皇将以虚拟方式出席仪式。利奥是第一位美国教皇。尽管总统发出了邀请，但他不会在美国建国250周年之际访问美国。

paragraphs.speaker: Audie Cornish
paragraphs.text: Leo will visit an Italian island that is an migrant hotspot on July 4th. And Senator Jon Ossoff almost didn't run for another Senate term, but now his campaign is in full swing and going viral online. New polling from Fox has Senator Ossoff out front with an early, double digit lead in Georgia's Senate race. Behind the scenes there's growing chatter that if Democrats are looking for their next national leader, Ossoff's name belongs on the short list. And the group chat is back to talk about it.
paragraphs.cn: 利奥将于7月4日访问一个移民热点意大利岛屿。参议员乔恩·奥索夫差点不再竞选连任，但现在他的竞选活动全面展开，并在网上走红。福克斯的最新民调显示，奥索夫参议员在佐治亚州参议员竞选中以两位数的早期领先优势领先。幕后有越来越多的议论，如果民主党正在寻找下一位全国领袖，奥索夫的名字应该出现在候选名单上。群聊又回来讨论这件事了。

paragraphs.speaker: Audie Cornish
paragraphs.text: Why? Because Isaac Dovere has been writing about this. You actually went down there to talk to Ossoff.
paragraphs.cn: 为什么？因为艾萨克·多维尔一直在写这个。你实际上去了那里和奥索夫谈话。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: What is he doing differently than the conversation we've been stuck having here? Democratic socialists on the rise. Moderates upset. Who is he in the spectrum?
paragraphs.cn: 他做的和我们一直在这里进行的对话有什么不同？民主社会主义者正在崛起。温和派感到不安。他在光谱中处于什么位置？

paragraphs.speaker: Dovere
paragraphs.text: Well, you know, I asked him about that and he said, I'm not really interested in getting into Democratic factions. I couldn't get him going on it. We sat for quite a while in Savannah last week, and then his rally in Savannah on Saturday that I attended. What he does talk about is not really the big base issues or the national issues that are coming up. Everything he does on the stump, and even in the conversation with me, he very carefully directs back toward the issue of corruption.
paragraphs.cn: 嗯，你知道，我问他这个问题，他说，我对介入民主党派系不感兴趣。我没能让他继续谈下去。上周我们在萨凡纳待了很长时间，然后周六我参加了他在萨凡纳的集会。他谈论的并不是那些重大的基础议题或即将出现的国家议题。他在竞选演讲中做的每一件事，甚至与我的对话中，都非常小心地将话题引回到腐败问题上。

paragraphs.speaker: Dovere
paragraphs.text: And not just talking about corruption --
paragraphs.cn: 而且不仅仅是谈论腐败 --  --

paragraphs.speaker: Ok. Dovere
paragraphs.text: And corruption of the Trump presidency. But also linking that very directly to how it affects people's lives. So, look, he had a line that got a lot of people laughing at the beginning of the rally where he said, oh, the Iranians closed the Strait of Hormuz again and so the -- his quote was, the president threw his toys out of the stroller and didn't pass the housing bill. But again, that's -- or didn't sign the housing bill. That's making it about how it's relevant to people's lives.
paragraphs.cn: 还有特朗普政府的腐败。同时也非常直接地将这与人们的生活如何受到影响联系起来。所以，你看，他在集会开始时说了一句话，让很多人笑了，他说，哦，伊朗人再次关闭了霍尔木兹海峡，所以 --  -- 他的原话是，总统把玩具扔出了婴儿车，没有通过住房法案。但同样，那是 --  -- 或者说没有签署住房法案。这把它与人们的生活如何相关联系起来。

paragraphs.speaker: Ok. Dovere
paragraphs.text: He talked about -- when he talks about the ballroom being constructed, he says, what's going on is donors who got tax cuts are writing checks to -- for the ballroom, but those tax cuts paid, Ossoff says, and it's true when you look at the math of it, for the subsidy cuts on Obamacare that made health care prices go up for people. And he's always talking about things like that, but also highlighting things that many people haven't been even thinking about when it comes to the questions around corruption.
paragraphs.cn: 他谈到 --  -- 当他谈到正在建造的舞厅时，他说，正在发生的是，获得减税的捐助者正在为舞厅开支票，但这些减税支付了，奥索夫说，而且当你查看数学计算时，确实如此，用于奥巴马医改的补贴削减使得人们的医疗保健价格上涨。他总是谈论这样的事情，但也强调许多人在考虑腐败问题时甚至没有想过的事情。

paragraphs.speaker: Ok. Dovere
paragraphs.text: There is, for example, in Albania, something going on right now called the flamingo revolution. And it's because Jared Kushner and Ivanka Trump have been trying to buy an island to develop a resort there. It is causing such protests in Albania that it may topple the government there. And yet it is not something that is being talked about here.
paragraphs.cn: 例如，在阿尔巴尼亚，现在正在发生一场所谓的火烈鸟革命。这是因为贾里德·库什纳和伊万卡·特朗普一直试图购买一个岛屿来开发度假村。这在阿尔巴尼亚引起了如此大的抗议，以至于可能推翻那里的政府。然而，这里却没有人谈论这件事。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Well, you know -- exactly. So let me bring it to this side of the table because I think that's what's interesting. Taking something like corruption, which is a big amorphous concept, I think, for a lot of people. Even I, on air, you know, I'm trying to explain, this is how this crypto deal works. This is how this contract works. It's complicated. So, what do you think of using that as a way in to the voter?
paragraphs.cn: 嗯，你知道 --  -- 正是如此。所以让我把话题带到这边，因为我认为这很有趣。把腐败这样的东西，我认为对很多人来说是一个模糊的大概念。即使是我，在直播中，你知道，我试图解释，这个加密货币交易是如何运作的。这个合同是如何运作的。这很复杂。那么，你认为用这个作为切入点来接触选民怎么样？

paragraphs.speaker: Xochitl Hinojosa
paragraphs.text: Yes. All the polling I have seen is that Americans care about corruption, but they really care about it when you talk about how this family or the Trump administration or this billionaire is getting rich and richer and richer either through deals, et cetera, and your average American is paying higher prices.
paragraphs.cn: 是的。我看过的所有民调都显示，美国人关心腐败，但当你谈到这个家族或特朗普政府或这个亿万富翁如何通过交易等变得越来越富，而普通美国人却在支付更高的价格时，他们真的很在意。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: So, they have to be connected to taking the hit financially.
paragraphs.cn: 所以，必须将他们与经济上的损失联系起来。

paragraphs.speaker: Hinojosa
paragraphs.text: They are taking the hit. They are doing something on the backs of the working people.
paragraphs.cn: 他们正在承受损失。他们正在以劳动人民为代价做某些事情。

paragraphs.speaker: Hinojosa
paragraphs.text: I will say that it's smart for him to talk about that because we all know that the number one issue is the economy. That's what American voters care about. But when you're looking at Ossoff moving forward and his potential future, the other thing you know about the Democratic Party is, they want a fighter. They don't want someone who's going to talk about love and unity in a primary.
paragraphs.cn: 我要说的是，他谈论这个很聪明，因为我们都知道头号问题是经济。这就是美国选民关心的。但当你看到奥索夫前进以及他潜在的未来时，你对民主党的另一了解是，他们想要一个斗士。他们不想要一个在初选中谈论爱与团结的人。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Are you sure? Because let's look at Ossoff's campaign image, the "o" for Ossoff looks suspiciously like, I don't know, an o that feels like one, that reminds one of hope and change.
paragraphs.cn: 你确定吗？因为让我们看看奥索夫的竞选形象，奥索夫名字中的"o"看起来可疑地像，我不知道，一个让人联想到希望与变革的"o"。

paragraphs.speaker: Jesse Arm
paragraphs.text: Three stipes through it.
paragraphs.cn: 上面有三条杠。

paragraphs.speaker: Hinojosa
paragraphs.text: But I will say -- but in the primary, yes, but it --
paragraphs.cn: 但我要说 --  -- 在初选中，是的，但它 --  --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: So, this is what the -- when they go low --
paragraphs.cn: 所以，这就是当 --  -- 他们降低姿态时 --  --

paragraphs.speaker: Hinojosa
paragraphs.text: That -- I don't think -- I think Democrats have retired that.
paragraphs.cn: 那 --  -- 我不认为 --  -- 我认为民主党已经放弃那个了。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: No, not so much. Let me let you get a word in.
paragraphs.cn: 不，没那么严重。让我让你说句话。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Are we looking at another Obama? And if you are, is that good or bad news for Republicans in the age of Trump? We're in a way different time now where you fight off a candidate like this with a Romney.
paragraphs.cn: 我们是在看另一个奥巴马吗？如果是，在特朗普时代这对共和党来说是好事还是坏事？我们现在处于一个完全不同的时代，你要用罗姆尼这样的人来对抗这样的候选人。

paragraphs.speaker: Arm
paragraphs.text: Ossoff has managed to maintain relatively inoffensive so far --
paragraphs.cn: 奥索夫到目前为止成功地保持了相对温和 --  --

paragraphs.speaker: Arm
paragraphs.text: To both the mainstream liberal majority of the Democratic coalition, as well as the ascendant far left flank.
paragraphs.cn: 既对民主党联盟中主流的自由派多数，也对崛起的极左翼。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: And we're going to show the cash on hand while you're speaking.
paragraphs.cn: 在你说话的时候，我们将展示手头的现金。

paragraphs.speaker: Arm
paragraphs.text: Yes. Yes. That works. Look, I think that Ossoff, ultimately the big challenge for him will be that the far left ascendant flank will continue to demand more and more and more. And as they extract that from him, there will be a price to play (ph) with the more moderate majority of the Democratic Party. Ultimately --
paragraphs.cn: 是的。是的。这样行。听着，我认为奥索夫最终面临的最大挑战是，极左翼势力会不断要求更多。当他们从他那里得到这些时，他将不得不与民主党中更温和的多数派付出代价。最终 --  --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: In a Senate race or do you mean for 2028?
paragraphs.cn: 在参议院竞选中，还是你指的是2028年？

paragraphs.speaker: Arm
paragraphs.text: Oh, no, no, no, I think he's going to win the Senate race.
paragraphs.cn: 哦，不，不，不，我认为他会赢得参议院竞选。

paragraphs.speaker: Ok. Arm
paragraphs.text: But we're already talking about 2028, and that's part of the --
paragraphs.cn: 但我们已经在谈论2028年了，这也是 --  --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Yes, I was going to say --
paragraphs.cn: 是的，我正要说 --  --

paragraphs.speaker: Arm
paragraphs.text: That's part of the allure of this guy. Republicans really had a damaging dynamic where the popular incumbent governor of Georgia endorsed one candidate. The president endorsed somebody else. They're both coming out of that bruised. You know, Mike Collins is going to be the nominee. This was supposed to be one of the biggest pickup opportunities for Republicans this cycle. And now Ossoff is poised to run away with it because he is having this viral moment. But we'll see if he can sustain it. I don't know that he --
paragraphs.cn: 这也是这个人的魅力所在。共和党人确实有一个破坏性的动态：佐治亚州受欢迎的现任州长支持了一位候选人，而总统支持了另一位。他们俩都因此受伤。你知道，迈克·柯林斯将成为提名人。这本来应该是共和党本周期最大的机会之一。而现在奥索夫因为他的病毒式传播时刻，准备轻松获胜。但我们会看他能否维持下去。我不知道他 --  --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Yes, viral without being all about the internet, which I find interesting as well.
paragraphs.cn: 是的，病毒式传播却不完全依赖互联网，我觉得这很有趣。

paragraphs.speaker: Dovere
paragraphs.text: Yes. And, look, he's done some of the things that are like basic stuff. People, Republicans -- from Republicans to people on the far left talk about his constituent services in Georgia. But it is really stunning. Georgia is a purple state at best. It's really like a red state that has elected some Democrats.
paragraphs.cn: 是的。而且，他做了一些基本的事情。人们 --  -- 从共和党人到极左派 --  -- 都在谈论他在佐治亚州的选民服务。但这确实令人震惊。佐治亚州充其量是个紫色州。它实际上更像是一个选举出一些民主党人的红州。

paragraphs.speaker: Dovere
paragraphs.text: And Ossoff is running comfortably there. Now. I asked him, does he think of himself as being in a strong position? He said, I'm the underdog. But look, you showed that money there. The polling that's there. It is -- it is striking to see.
paragraphs.cn: 而奥索夫在那里竞选得很轻松。现在，我问他，他是否认为自己处于强势地位？他说，我是弱者。但你看，你展示了那些资金。那里的民调。这 --  -- 这很引人注目。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Yes, the vibe is not underdog.
paragraphs.cn: 是的，感觉不像弱者。

paragraphs.speaker: Dovere
paragraphs.text: They're -- the last time a Democrat was elected to a second full term in the Senate from Georgia was Sam Nunn in 1990.
paragraphs.cn: 上一次民主党人从佐治亚州当选连任参议员还是1990年的萨姆·纳恩。

paragraphs.speaker: Ok. Dovere
paragraphs.text: And this race is being thought of by Democrats as basically a shoo-in at this point. We'll see if it remains that way. But it is a real testament to what Ossoff has been able to pull off. And the way that, you know, he gets this -- he has -- he said to me no on the presidential stuff. He -- it comes because he pops up once a month with these speeches and gets people talking.
paragraphs.cn: 目前民主党人认为这场竞选基本上稳操胜券。我们拭目以待是否如此。但这确实证明了奥索夫的能力。而且，你知道，他 --  -- 他对我说总统的事不表态。他 --  -- 这是因为每个月他都会发表演讲，引发人们讨论。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: OK. Well, we love a good, he's running story. So, I'm sure we'll be back. I want to turn back to America's 250th birthday. The country is, frankly, still arguing over its founding. We've gone from debates about confederate monuments, to fights over critical race theory and diversity, equity, inclusion, and how history should be taught in schools. And now there's this new Gallup poll showing just 33 percent of U.S.
paragraphs.cn: 好的。我们喜欢听他的竞选故事。我相信我们还会再聊。我想回到美国250岁生日的话题。坦率地说，这个国家仍在为建国问题争论不休。从关于邦联纪念碑的辩论，到围绕批判性种族理论、多样性、公平、包容以及学校历史教育的斗争。现在盖洛普最新民调显示，只有33%的美国

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: adults are extremely proud to be an American, but that is the lowest level recorded on that question in 25 years. So, what does that tell us about where America is in its own story? So, I'm bringing in Leah Wright, CNN political historian and associate professor of history at Johns Hopkin's University, and Alexander Heffner, host of the PBS show "The Open Mind." He's also the co-author of a documentary, "History of the United States." So, I wanted to talk to you guys because with this Trump administration, they
paragraphs.cn: 成年人以身为美国人为荣，这是25年来该问题的最低记录。那么，这告诉我们美国在其自身故事中处于什么位置？因此，我请来了CNN政治历史学家、约翰·霍普金斯大学历史学副教授莉亚·赖特，以及PBS节目《开放思想》的主持人亚历山大·赫夫纳。他也是纪录片《美国历史》的合著者。我想和你们聊聊，因为特朗普政府

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: have made restoring pride in our country actually a kind of guiding light. When you look at some of the executive orders, for instance, one of the things they do is try to correct for what they see as too much dogma in politics.
paragraphs.cn: 实际上将恢复国家自豪感作为指导原则。看看一些行政命令，例如，他们试图纠正他们认为政治中过多的教条。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: One of them saying, they want to "instead focus on the greatness of the achievements and progress of the American people, respect of natural features, beauty, abundance, and grandeur of the American landscape." If that's the case, and I want to start with you, Leah, why are we seeing this Gallup poll number and pride actually dip further?
paragraphs.cn: 其中一项命令说，他们希望"转而关注美国人民的伟大成就和进步，尊重自然特征、美丽、富饶和壮丽的美国景观。"如果是这样，我想先问你，莉亚，为什么我们看到盖洛普民调数字和自豪感反而进一步下降？

paragraphs.speaker: Leah Wright
paragraphs.text: Well, I think one of the things to point out here is that the Trump administration's actions are having the exact opposite effect, at least amongst the public, and how the public understands and wants to understand American history and America at 250. Now, for the Trump administration, this is their vision of what America should be. And that's not unusual.
paragraphs.cn: 嗯，我认为需要指出的一点是，特朗普政府的行动产生了完全相反的效果，至少在公众中，以及公众如何理解和想要理解美国历史以及250岁的美国。对特朗普政府来说，这是他们对美国应有的愿景。这并不罕见。

paragraphs.speaker: Leah Wright
paragraphs.text: We've had a lot of -- you know, we've had a lot of work, a lot of ,scholarship that shows us that arounds -- that around America's anniversaries there tends to be a push from administrations to whitewash American history, to focus simply on the great acts, but a very specific great acts by specific great people, predominantly white men. But that's not the mood that Americans are in today. Americans want to hear about all of the messy, complicated aspects of American history, of United States history.
paragraphs.cn: 我们有很多 --  -- 你知道，我们有很多研究、很多学术成果表明，在美国周年纪念日前后，政府往往会推动粉饰美国历史，只关注伟大的行为，但只是特定伟人的特定伟大行为，主要是白人男性。但这并不是今天美国人的情绪。美国人想听到美国历史、合众国历史中所有混乱、复杂的方面。

paragraphs.speaker: Leah Wright
paragraphs.text: They understand just simply by virtue of their experience within the United States. Even the experience today of, you know, of inequality, lack of social mobility.
paragraphs.cn: 他们仅仅通过在美国的经历就理解了这一点。即使是今天的经历，你知道，不平等、缺乏社会流动性。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Yes. But can I jump here on that because --
paragraphs.cn: 是的。但我可以插一句吗，因为 --  --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: I do wonder, what you're talking about is this feeling that the social contract is frayed, right?
paragraphs.cn: 我确实在想，你所说的是一种社会契约被削弱的感觉，对吧？

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: That somehow the -- we're not holding up our end of the bargain here as institutions. I want to come to you, Alex, because I'm looking at this polling where it says, do you think the American dream still holds true? Fifty-one percent said once held true but no longer. Just 15 percent said never held true. So, I don't think I'm looking at a populist that is like, the American dream is always BS.
paragraphs.cn: 不知怎的，我们作为机构没有履行自己的承诺。我想问你，亚历克斯，因为我看到这个民调说，你认为美国梦仍然成立吗？51%的人说曾经成立但不再成立。只有15%的人说从未成立。所以，我不认为我看到的是一个民粹主义者说，美国梦一直是扯淡。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: There's plenty of people who seem to have thought, yes, there was a lot of things sort of promised and being here and we don't think we're trending in that direction. ALEXANDER HEFFNER, HOST, PBS "THE OPEN MIND": Audie, I call this a kind of delinquency of futility, or you could also call it the futility of delinquency in American politics. Many people have commented on the paralysis as a result of the partizan division, the ad hominems, the viciousness of the discourse. And it's not even that it's so bellicose.
paragraphs.cn: 有很多人似乎认为，是的，有很多事情被承诺了，但我们觉得并没有朝着那个方向发展。PBS《开放思想》主持人亚历山大·赫夫纳：奥迪，我称之为一种徒劳的失职，或者你也可以称之为美国政治中失职的徒劳。许多人评论过由于党派分歧、人身攻击和恶毒言论导致的瘫痪。而且这不仅仅是好战的问题。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: It's that it has strung us up in a constant state of impasse that we can't feel the achievement of the present. And I always think it's important to return to those founding documents and to hold them up for both the aspirations and the resentments that we experience and have experienced as a country. But I think part of the problem is the gaslighting.
paragraphs.cn: 它让我们陷入持续的僵局，无法感受到当下的成就。我始终认为，回归那些建国文献很重要，把它们作为我们作为一个国家所经历和正在经历的渴望和怨恨的参照。但我认为部分问题在于煤气灯效应。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: You know, when you look at something like the Emoluments Clause, earlier you were talking about corruption in the Trump administration, and the fact that the United States Supreme Court, during the Trump administration's first go, essentially said, that doesn't exist. And now we have a president who's flying on a plane, in Air Force One, recently debuted that is a gift from Qatar. So, there are things that just don't add up in the republic right now.
paragraphs.cn: 你知道，当你看到像《薪酬条款》这样的东西时，早些时候你在谈论特朗普政府的腐败，以及美国最高法院在特朗普政府第一任期时基本上说那不存在。而现在我们的总统乘坐的空军一号飞机是卡塔尔赠送的礼物。所以，这个共和国现在有些事情就是不对劲。

paragraphs.speaker: Heffner
paragraphs.text: And let's just be honest about this. We have to be equal opportunity critics. And the golden rule of American political life ought to be intellectual honesty. So, when people talk about Speaker Pelosi --
paragraphs.cn: 让我们坦诚面对这一点。我们必须成为平等的批评者。美国政治生活的黄金法则应该是知识上的诚实。所以，当人们谈论议长佩洛西时 --  --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Well, let me jump in here then --
paragraphs.cn: 好吧，那我插一句 --  --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Just because you're in the modern politics, and I brought you guys on because it's been a time for historians in the last couple of years. I imagine your group chats are all a mess. And I remember a few years ago when the 1619 Project came out, we never heard the end of it. The backlash, the arguing, what should be taught in schools. As we speak, people are talking about textbooks. I think the Trump administration, and many supporters have pushed back harder on 1776 as the time.
paragraphs.cn: 仅仅因为你身处现代政治中，我请你们来是因为过去几年是历史学家的时代。我想你们的群聊一定一团糟。我记得几年前当1619项目出来时，我们没完没了地听到它。反弹、争论、学校应该教什么。就在我们说话的时候，人们还在讨论教科书。我认为特朗普政府和许多支持者更强烈地反击了1776年作为起点。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Very quick to you both, to your mind, what year do you see as historians yourself? Is there a particular era you look at and think, that was the real start of America. It doesn't have to be either of those two numbers, or it can be. And can I go to you, Leah.
paragraphs.cn: 快速问你们两位，作为历史学家，你们认为哪一年是美国的真正开端？有没有一个特定的时代让你们觉得那是美国的真正开始？不一定非要是那两个年份，也可以是别的。我能先问你吗，莉亚？

paragraphs.speaker: Wright
paragraphs.text: 1852 Frederick Douglass' speech, July 5th speech on the anniversary of the 76th anniversary of the Declaration -- signing of the Declaration of Independence, because it's Douglass who's the first person to really call out in a really concentrated way this idea of the hypocrisy of liberty, a nation for liberty and justice for all that is intimately and violently rejecting that premise for the bulk of the people. So, that becomes the story of the nation. That's the one that we remember.
paragraphs.cn: 1852年弗雷德里克·道格拉斯的演讲，7月5日在《独立宣言》签署76周年纪念日上的演讲，因为道格拉斯是第一个真正集中地指出这种自由虚伪的人，一个宣称人人享有自由和正义的国家却亲密而暴力地拒绝这一前提对大多数人适用。所以，这成了这个国家的故事。这是我们记住的那个故事。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: OK, that's one for you. Every historian has one. Alexander Heffner, for you, when do you think this country began?
paragraphs.cn: 好的，这是给你的问题。每个历史学家都有一个。亚历山大·赫夫纳，你认为这个国家始于何时？

paragraphs.speaker: Heffner
paragraphs.text: Yes. 1776, we didn't achieve the blessings of liberty -- life, liberty and happiness for some decades and centuries. But I think that the founding period is indisputable as a matter of fact. I think part of what created the controversy of reevaluating that was, we have to be true to facts. So, if we're talking about the founding of this new nation, birthed from our mother country, England, it was 1776 and the Continental Congress.
paragraphs.cn: 是的。1776年，我们在几十年甚至几个世纪后才实现了自由的福祉 --  -- 生命、自由和幸福。但我认为建国时期作为一个事实是无可争议的。我认为重新评估这一点的争议部分源于我们必须忠于事实。所以，如果我们谈论这个从母国英国诞生的新国家的建立，那就是1776年和大陆会议。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: OK. Well, here from the desk where we write the rough draft of history, usually, I want to thank you guys for being here. I hope you have a good Independence Day holiday.
paragraphs.cn: 好的。那么，在这个我们通常撰写历史初稿的地方，我想感谢你们的到来。希望你们独立日假期愉快。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: And we're going to kick off America 250 with Anderson Cooper and Andy Cohen as they count down to midnight for historic ball drop honoring the nation's birthday. "Independence Eve Live with Anderson Cooper and Andy Cohen." Coverage begins tonight at 8 p.m. on CNN or, of course, stream it on the CNN app. Next, we're going to be talking about the president on defense, why he says he did nothing wrong when it comes to the profits he has made in the crypto industry. Later on CNN, your holiday travel.
paragraphs.cn: 我们将与安德森·库珀和安迪·科恩一起启动美国250周年庆典，他们将进行午夜倒计时，迎接纪念国家诞生的历史性球体降落。"独立日前夜直播：安德森·库珀与安迪·科恩"。报道将于今晚8点在CNN开始，当然也可以在CNN应用程序上观看。接下来，我们将讨论总统的辩护，为什么他说自己在加密货币行业获利方面没有做错任何事。稍后在CNN，关注您的假日旅行。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: We know you're on the road. And we're going to be live from Atlanta's airport. Millions of Americans plan to head out this Fourth.
paragraphs.cn: 我们知道您正在路上。我们将从亚特兰大机场进行现场直播。数百万美国人计划在这个七月四日出行。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: So, the president, not shying away from the fact that he made more than $2 billion in his first year back in the White House. And this includes more than $1.4 billion in crypto ventures. When asked about it on CNBC where he spoke, the president says, look, there's nothing to see here.
paragraphs.cn: 所以，总统并不回避他重返白宫第一年赚取超过20亿美元的事实，其中包括超过14亿美元的加密货币投资。当他在CNBC接受采访时被问及此事，总统说，看，这里没什么可看的。

paragraphs.speaker: Joe Kernan
paragraphs.text: In the disclosure this week the amount of money that you and the family made in crypto, it was an outsized number. I was just asking, did you -- were you in -- know about the crypto ventures?
paragraphs.cn: 在本周的披露中，您和家人在加密货币上赚取的金额是一个巨大的数字。我只是想问，您是否 --  -- 您是否了解这些加密货币投资？

paragraphs.speaker: Kernan
paragraphs.text: So, that was just something --
paragraphs.cn: 所以，那只是 --  --

paragraphs.speaker: Trump
paragraphs.text: I could know about it.
paragraphs.cn: 我可能知道。

paragraphs.speaker: Trump
paragraphs.text: I mean, there's nothing illegal. There's nothing wrong with it.
paragraphs.cn: 我的意思是，这没什么违法的。这没什么不对的。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Group chat is back. I wanted to play this because we made such a deal out of it yesterday. And he actually got to speak. And I think, interestingly, with a business outlet, right? These are guys. They're no -- they're not foreign to outsized paydays. What do you make of how he's defending it?
paragraphs.cn: 群聊又回来了。我想播放这个，因为我们昨天对此大做文章。而且他实际上有机会发言。我认为，有趣的是，面对一个商业媒体，对吧？这些人 --  -- 他们对巨额薪酬并不陌生。你怎么看他为自己辩护的方式？

paragraphs.speaker: Dovere
paragraphs.text: Do most Americans know people who made $1.4 billion, $2 billion over the last 15 months or 16 months? I think that that's really what this comes down to for a lot of people. It's, do most Americans know people whose sons got a good deal on a tungsten mine in Kazakhstan? No. What most Americans are seeing is prices that they are fighting with and struggling with. And that is the real --
paragraphs.cn: 大多数美国人认识在过去15或16个月里赚了14亿、20亿美元的人吗？我认为这确实是很多人关注的核心问题。大多数美国人认识那些儿子在哈萨克斯坦钨矿上捡到便宜的人吗？不。大多数美国人看到的是他们正在与之斗争和挣扎的价格。这才是真正的 --  --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Yes. We were playing tape yesterday where Megyn Kelly was like, this is grifty (ph). I don't like what the sons are doing. I don't like -- so there's this world of people who were the Hunter Biden hunters who are now, like, OK, guys, wait a second, this doesn't feel good that we have to defend it. But I do want to bring up this one thing. Mary Trump, longtime Trump critic, but she was on "AC360" and she brought up this issue, which I had not thought about. MARY TRUMP, Donald
paragraphs.cn: 是的。我们昨天播放了梅根·凯利的片段，她说，这很可疑。我不喜欢儿子们的行为。我不喜欢 --  -- 所以有一群曾经是亨特·拜登猎手的人，现在却说，好吧，伙计们，等等，这感觉不太好，我们不得不为之辩护。但我确实想提一件事。玛丽·特朗普，长期批评特朗普的人，她上了《AC360》节目，提出了这个问题，我之前没想到过。玛丽·特朗普，唐纳德 --  --

paragraphs.speaker: Trump'S Neice
paragraphs.text: The problem here is not so much the ways in which Donald and his family continue to rig the system in their favor, it's the number of people who are willing either to look the other way or to enable it. This is the president of the United States endangering the national security of American citizens because he is willing to take money from any foreign entity, no matter how potentially hostile it might be to American interests.
paragraphs.cn: 问题不在于唐纳德和他的家人继续以对自己有利的方式操纵体制，而在于有多少人愿意视而不见或纵容这种行为。这是美国总统在危害美国公民的国家安全，因为他愿意从任何外国实体拿钱，无论该实体对美国利益可能有多么潜在的敌意。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: OK. I'd like to fact check that. I can't, because, guess what, the IRS is barred from ever looking at the Trump family financial records in terms of audits. It says in that last deal they made, "plaintiffs hereby release, waive, acquit and forever discharge defendants and are hereby forever barred and precluded from prosecuting or pursuing any claims." We might not actually know the sources of some of this money.
paragraphs.cn: 好的。我想核实一下这个说法。但我不能，因为猜怎么着，国税局被禁止查看特朗普家族的财务记录，就审计而言。在他们达成的最后一项协议中写道："原告特此免除、放弃、解除并永久免除被告，并特此永久禁止和阻止提出或追究任何索赔。"我们可能实际上不知道其中一些钱的来源。

paragraphs.speaker: Arm
paragraphs.text: Yes. I mean most Americans don't know people who have made billions of dollars off of crypto. Most Americans also don't know the president of the United States. But most Americans also are totally willing to accept the fact that Trump is a --
paragraphs.cn: 是的。我的意思是，大多数美国人并不认识那些通过加密货币赚了数十亿美元的人。大多数美国人也不认识美国总统。但大多数美国人也完全愿意接受特朗普是一个 --  --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: But they're also not running Mideast policy, right? No, no, pause for a second.
paragraphs.cn: 但他们也不负责中东政策，对吧？不，不，暂停一下。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: If you're in charge of policy, isn't that what's supposed to make it distinct?
paragraphs.cn: 如果你负责政策，这不正是应该让它与众不同的地方吗？

paragraphs.speaker: Arm
paragraphs.text: Yes, well, I think, again, most Americans sort of take the effort to be rich, stay rich, get richer with Donald Trump baked into the equation. I don't think anybody who voted for him or voted against him in 2024 is going to be shocked by these developments. This is kind of (INAUDIBLE) response.
paragraphs.cn: 是的，嗯，我认为，再说一次，大多数美国人基本上把唐纳德·特朗普努力致富、保持富有、变得更富视为理所当然。我认为任何在2024年投票支持或反对他的人都不会对这些事态感到震惊。这基本上是一种（听不清）反应。

paragraphs.speaker: Dovere
paragraphs.text: I mean he said years ago, long before he ever ran for president, I'm the only person who could run for president and actually make money on it. So, this is part of the equation.
paragraphs.cn: 我的意思是，他多年前，早在竞选总统之前就说过，我是唯一一个可以竞选总统并从中赚钱的人。所以，这是其中的一部分。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Yes. Well, promises made, promises kept.
paragraphs.cn: 是的。嗯，承诺已做，承诺已兑现。

paragraphs.speaker: Arm
paragraphs.text: Yes, and he talked about making money from foreign adversaries. I do think that is something that Trump looks at the balance sheet and sort of says, let's have these guys pay us, rather than us doling out money to the rest of the world. And certain populist --
paragraphs.cn: 是的，他还谈到要从外国对手那里赚钱。我确实认为特朗普会看资产负债表，然后说，让这些人付钱给我们，而不是我们向世界其他地方施舍。某些民粹主义者 --  --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Xochitl, let me let you get a word in here. I love when these men are talking, but just in case.
paragraphs.cn: Xochitl，让我插句话。我喜欢听这些男人说话，但以防万一。

paragraphs.speaker: Hinajosa
paragraphs.text: Yes. No, I -- Donald Trump ran for president in what it seems like now for two reasons. One, it was a get out of jail free card because you cannot prosecute a sitting president. Two is to essentially benefit financially off the presidency.
paragraphs.cn: 是的。不，我 --  -- 唐纳德·特朗普竞选总统现在看来有两个原因。一是这是一张免狱卡，因为你不能起诉在任总统。二是基本上从总统职位上获得经济利益。

paragraphs.speaker: Hinajosa
paragraphs.text: The difference, while he might say it is not illegal, the difference is that, if this were a Democratic president and there was a Republican Congress, you would have oversight -- the Oversight Committee and all sorts of oversight of his family about their -- the financial dealings within this administration under potential contracts.
paragraphs.cn: 区别在于，虽然他可能说这不违法，但区别在于，如果这是一位民主党总统，而共和党控制国会，你会有监督 --  -- 监督委员会以及各种对其家族的监督，关于他们在潜在合同下与本届政府内的财务往来。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Yes. Well, let me end there because --
paragraphs.cn: 是的。嗯，我就此打住，因为 --  --

paragraphs.speaker: Arm
paragraphs.text: (INAUDIBLE) Congress well have that.
paragraphs.cn: （听不清）国会会有那个。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Yes, I was about to say, we're heading to the midterms, so you may get your wish. Let me do group chats because Alex is new to the group chat. And I need to understand your vibe. So, what are people talking about in your world?
paragraphs.cn: 是的，我正要说到，我们正走向中期选举，所以你的愿望可能会实现。让我来做群聊，因为Alex是群聊新手。我需要了解你的氛围。那么，在你的世界里，人们在谈论什么？

paragraphs.speaker: Arm
paragraphs.text: Yes. Well, I've been reading this new Dave Portnoy book, which is kind of a best seller now.
paragraphs.cn: 是的。嗯，我一直在读Dave Portnoy的新书，现在算是畅销书。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: He talked about maybe running for office, Dave Portnoy.
paragraphs.cn: 他谈到可能竞选公职，Dave Portnoy。

paragraphs.speaker: Arm
paragraphs.text: You took the words out of my mouth.
paragraphs.cn: 你把我要说的话说了。

paragraphs.speaker: Arm
paragraphs.text: This is the other thing sort of in my group chat. Actually more so than the book, because as Portnoy says in his book, books are for losers and overrated anyway.
paragraphs.cn: 这其实是我群聊里的另一件事。实际上比那本书更甚，因为正如波特诺伊在他的书中所说，书是给失败者看的，而且无论如何都被高估了。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Sure. Sure. Open (INAUDIBLE).
paragraphs.cn: 当然。当然。打开（听不清）。

paragraphs.speaker: Arm
paragraphs.text: He's talking about running for mayor of New York City against Zohran Mamdani.
paragraphs.cn: 他正在谈论竞选纽约市市长，对手是佐兰·马姆达尼。

paragraphs.speaker: Arm
paragraphs.text: That was something he made headlines with this week. Curtis Sliwa bit back, I think saw someone running into his lane potentially there. So, you know --
paragraphs.cn: 这是他本周登上头条的事情。柯蒂斯·斯利瓦反击了，我想是看到有人可能闯入了他的赛道。所以，你知道 --  --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Someone got a beret and they started the starting gun.
paragraphs.cn: 有人戴上了贝雷帽，然后他们打响了发令枪。

paragraphs.speaker: Arm
paragraphs.text: It should be interesting.
paragraphs.cn: 应该会很有趣。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Xochitl, what about you?
paragraphs.cn: 索奇特尔，你呢？

paragraphs.speaker: Hinajosa
paragraphs.text: So, my group chat is actually called "shake it off." And it is all about Taylor.
paragraphs.cn: 我的群聊实际上叫"甩掉它"。全是关于泰勒的。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Xochitl -- of course.
paragraphs.cn: 索奇特尔 --  -- 当然。

paragraphs.speaker: Hinojosa
paragraphs.text: And it is all about Taylor Swift. We already covered it. But I want to say, people are wondering who is invited. More importantly, who is not invited to the wedding. Things like the dress, what the decor looks like, all those fun things.
paragraphs.cn: 这一切都围绕着泰勒·斯威夫特。我们已经报道过了。但我想说，人们都在好奇谁被邀请了。更重要的是，谁没有被邀请参加婚礼。还有像礼服、装饰风格等等这些有趣的事情。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: OK. OK. We'll come back to it tomorrow. Isaac, (INAUDIBLE).
paragraphs.cn: 好的，好的。我们明天再谈这个。艾萨克，（听不清）。

paragraphs.speaker: Dovere
paragraphs.text: I mean I guess pretty -- it's the World Cup. This game tonight with Argentina and Cape Verde and what is going to happen there. Could there be a major upset? Perhaps. Or will it just be like a showcase for Lionel Messi to do all of his stuff?
paragraphs.cn: 我的意思是，我想这很 --  -- 这是世界杯。今晚阿根廷对阵佛得角的比赛，会发生什么呢？会不会有大冷门？也许吧。或者这仅仅是莱昂内尔·梅西展示他所有技巧的舞台？

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Love the Cinderella story. Love it. Yes. Baby, just say yes. I'm Audie Cornish. The headlines are next. Thank you so much for being with us here on CNN. var CNN = CNN || {};
paragraphs.cn: 喜欢这个灰姑娘的故事。太喜欢了。是的，宝贝，就答应吧。我是奥蒂·科尼什。接下来是新闻头条。非常感谢您收看CNN。
